_meta.author: ChrisPachulski
_meta.purpose: Cross-language byte-equality proof for HKDF-SHA256 with zero-length salt, 32-byte OKM. The single sentence in keyDerivation.ts claiming 'Rust b"eex/session/v1" produces identical bytes' is otherwise unverifiable; this vector locks the equivalence.

algorithm.name: HKDF
algorithm.salt_note: Zero-length salt per RFC 5869 §2.2. Node: hkdfSync('sha256', ikm, '', info, 32). Rust hkdf crate: Hkdf::<Sha256>::new(None, ikm). Swift CryptoKit: HKDF.deriveKey(inputKeyMaterial: ikm, salt: Data(), info: info, outputByteCount: 32).

info_strings.stream_token_reserved: eex/stream-token/v1 (NOT used — stream tokens use raw env-var bytes per contract D18 amendment)

negative_checks.name: different-info-produces-different-okm
negative_checks.assertion: For the same IKM, derivations under different info strings MUST produce different OKM. This is what 'domain separation' means.

negative_checks.name: same-input-deterministic
negative_checks.assertion: Two derivations of the same (IKM, info) pair MUST produce identical OKM bytes. HKDF is deterministic; any divergence indicates a bug in the underlying SHA-256 or HMAC implementation.
